# Title: Directive on Administrative Cooperation–7th Amendment (DAC7)

Title: Directive on Administrative Cooperation–7th Amendment (DAC7)

URL Source: https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/GM472VUSJDLG2HSB

Markdown Content:
#### Is DAC7 a new tax?

No. DAC7is an EU directive (EU Council Directive 2021/514) setting out transparency rules that applies to all digital operators, such as Amazon.

#### My primary address is in the EU but I only sell outside of the EU. Am I affected?

Yes. DAC7 affects sellers with a primary address in the EU, irrespective of the store in which they sell. For example, a seller with a primary address in Italy who only sells in Amazon’s US store will be affected if their sales on that store exceed €2,000 or they have completed 30 or more transactions within the current or any following calendar year.

#### My primary address is in Germany, and I’m active in the France, Spain, and Germany stores. Am I required to add the necessary information three times?

No. The EU is considered a single store, so you must only add the necessary information once in the EU Seller Central account of your choice.

#### My primary address is in Germany, and I’m active in the Germany, France and Mexico stores. How many times must I add the necessary information?

You must add the required information twice, once in your Mexico Seller Central account and once in the EU store of your choice. For example, if you add and verify the information only for the EU but not for Mexico, your Mexico account would be non-compliant. Your non-compliant account in Mexico would then be deactivated and funds would be withheld accordingly.

#### Where will I get the first notice from Amazon?

You'll get a notification in your Performance Notifications dashboard. To access the notification, select **Performance** in the main Seller Central menu.

#### Should I wait to get the performance notification to comply with DAC7?

#### Should I wait to get the performance notification to comply with DAC7?

No. You can submit DAC7 via the [DAC7 compliance portal](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/complianceportal?ref=DAC7_hp_D7port) at any time.

#### Can I save my information and complete the form later?

Yes. The update wizard allows you to resume entering data at any step in the process. Make sure that you click **Submit** on each screen after entering your information. You’ll still be able to use Seller Central. However, if you don’t provide the required information within 60 days from the first performance notification, your selling privileges will be suspended. Your privileges may be reinstated once you’ve entered the required information and we’ve verified it.

#### Why is DAC7 validation taking so long? DAC7 status shows as 'Under review.' How can we avoid deactivation?

#### Why is DAC7 validation taking so long? DAC7 status shows as 'Under review.' How can we avoid deactivation?

If it’s been more than 14 days and the DAC7 validation status is still pending, check to see if the legal name and business address that you provided are correct and amend the information if needed.

#### **How can I check the status of the information that I submitted?**

You can check the status of your submission at the top of the [DAC7 compliance portal](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/complianceportal?ref=DAC7_hp_D7port) under **Compliance status**. The statuses are as follows:

*   **At risk**: You must still review, confirm, and submit your DAC7 information on the DAC7 compliance portal.
*   **Pending verification**: The information that you provided is being validated. VAT validation can take up to 14 calendar days. Identity validation can take up to 60 days depending on verification complexity and additional document required from you.
*   **Compliant**: We’ve validated the information that you provided, and no further action is needed.
*   **Not required at this time**: You’re not required to provide this information at this time. You’ll get a performance notification once you’re required to comply, or you can opt to provide the data before you get the notification.

If you need to update your business identity information, go to your Seller Central settings, select **Account info**, and then select **Business information**. To check the VAT registration numbers linked to your selling account and their status, go to your Seller Central settings, select **Account info**, and then select **Tax information**. Both sections must be fully verified to comply with DAC7.

#### My account has been deactivated. What should I do?

If your account has been deactivated, go to your Seller Central settings, select **Account info**, and look for the sections that are highlighted in red, as those are the areas requiring your attention. To reinstate your account, you must complete the required steps. We may have sent additional information about your account deactivation to your Performance Notifications dashboard.

#### I’ve already provided the requested information. Why is Amazon asking for more?

#### I’ve already provided the requested information. Why is Amazon asking for more?

During the verification process, we may determine that we need more information from you to complete your account verification. Also, we may require more details if there’s a change in your account circumstances or as part of periodic inspections to ensure ongoing compliance.

#### Why can't I edit my information?

You’re not able to update information while it’s being reviewed.

#### What if I need to update my information?

You can update your information by going to your Seller Central settings, selecting **Account info**, and clicking on the relevant section. If your information has already been verified or the verification process hasn’t yet started, you can make updates. However, all updates are subject to verification, and you may be asked to provide more information based on the results of the verification.

#### How will I be notified if Amazon requires additional information?

#### How will I be notified if Amazon requires additional information?

If we need more information, we’ll notify you via the email address that you provided during registration and a notification in Seller Central. You can also check for required updates by going to your Seller Central settings, selecting **Account info**, and looking for the sections that are highlighted in red. We may provide additional information about the request on your Performance Notifications dashboard.
